description:
## Goal
Headless Claude integration: spawn claude --print with structured JSON output, system prompt for drift analysis, and response parsing.

## Deliverables
- src/analysis/runner.ts: ClaudeRunner class
  - spawn() method: claude --print --output-format json --json-schema <schema> --model <model> --no-session-persistence --permission-mode bypassPermissions --system-prompt <prompt>
  - Pipe conversation data via stdin
  - Parse stdout as JSON, validate with zod
  - Handle errors (process exit codes, invalid JSON, timeouts)
- src/analysis/schema.ts: Zod schemas for Finding[] output from Claude, matching the findings table structure
- src/analysis/prompt.ts: System prompt that instructs Claude to:
  - Spawn explorer subagents to read conversation files
  - Detect: repeated mistakes, ignored instructions, over-engineering, security issues, anti-patterns, hallucinated APIs, inefficient tool usage
  - Extract: source, model, project, session date, severity, evidence excerpts, tool usage context
  - Output structured JSON matching the schema
- src/analysis/index.ts: analyze(conversations: NormalizedConversation[]) -> Finding[]

## Acceptance Criteria
- ClaudeRunner spawns claude process and captures JSON output
- Output validates against zod schema
- System prompt produces meaningful findings from a sample conversation
- Handles claude not found (clear error)
- Handles timeout (configurable, default 5 minutes per batch)
- Handles invalid JSON response (retry once, then error)
- Unit tests with mocked claude process
- Integration test with real claude --print (manual, not CI)
title: E4: Analysis Engine

description:
## Goal
Dolt database lifecycle management: server start/stop, schema creation, and CRUD operations for all tables.

## Deliverables
- src/storage/dolt.ts: DoltServer class with start(), stop(), isRunning(), ensureRunning(), connect() methods
  - Port derivation: FNV-32a hash of ~/.drift-watch/, range 14307-15306
  - PID file tracking at ~/.drift-watch/dolt-server.pid
  - Auto-start on first use
- src/storage/schema.sql: CREATE TABLE statements for scans, findings, patterns, finding_patterns, suggestions
- src/storage/migrations.ts: Schema versioning (version table, apply migrations on connect)
- src/storage/repository.ts: Repository class with CRUD methods for each table
- src/commands/init.ts: drift-watch init command that creates ~/.drift-watch/, initializes Dolt, runs schema, verifies dolt + claude CLIs
- src/commands/status.ts: drift-watch status command showing DB stats, last scan, server status

## Acceptance Criteria
- drift-watch init creates ~/.drift-watch/dolt/ and all schema tables
- drift-watch init checks dolt --version and claude --version, errors if missing
- drift-watch status shows table row counts and last scan timestamp
- DoltServer starts/stops Dolt process, survives parent exit (detached)
- Repository can insert/query/update all 5 tables
- Unit tests for repository CRUD (using test database)
- Integration test for init + status cycle

## Dependencies
- mysql2 npm package for MySQL protocol connection to Dolt
- ulid for ID generation
title: E3: Dolt Storage Layer

description:
## Overview
npm global package that analyzes AI coding conversations (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) for behavioral drift, repeated mistakes, and anti-patterns. Stores findings in a Dolt DB at ~/.drift-watch/, uses headless Claude (--print) with explorer subagents for analysis, and generates corrective strategy suggestions.

## Architecture
- **CLI**: commander-based TypeScript CLI installed globally via npm
- **Readers**: Parse conversation logs from ~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl (Claude), ~/.codex/sessions/**/*.jsonl + state_5.sqlite (Codex), ~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats/*.json (Gemini)
- **Analysis**: Headless claude --print --output-format json --json-schema with explorer subagents
- **Storage**: Own Dolt SQL server at ~/.drift-watch/dolt/ (port range 14307-15306, MySQL protocol via mysql2)
- **Output**: Pattern reports + AI-generated corrective suggestions (CLAUDE.md patches, linter rules, test cases, docs, training)

## CLI Commands
- drift-watch init: Initialize ~/.drift-watch/ + Dolt DB + schema
- drift-watch scan: Read recent conversations, analyze with headless Claude, store findings
- drift-watch report [--by-model|--by-project|--since]: Query and display patterns
- drift-watch suggest: Generate corrective strategies via headless Claude
- drift-watch cron install|remove|status: Manage periodic scan cron job
- drift-watch status: DB stats, last scan, Dolt server status

## Data Model (Dolt Tables)
- scans: id, started_at, completed_at, status, findings_count, cursor_claude/codex/gemini
- findings: id, scan_id, source, model, project, session_id, session_date, category, severity, title, description, evidence, tool_context
- patterns: id, category, title, description, occurrence_count, first_seen, last_seen, severity, status
- finding_patterns: finding_id, pattern_id (junction)
- suggestions: id, pattern_id, strategy_type, title, description, artifact, status, created_at

## Categories
repeated_mistake, ignored_instruction, over_engineering, security, anti_pattern, hallucinated_api, inefficient_tools

## Strategy Types
claude_md_patch, linter_rule, test_case, documentation, user_training

## Tech Stack
TypeScript, tsup, commander, vitest, mysql2 (Dolt), zod, ulid

## Key Design Decisions
- ADR-001: Own Dolt instance (not reusing beads server) -- clean separation, no beads dependency
- ADR-002: Single global DB (not per-project) -- enables cross-project pattern detection
- ADR-003: Headless claude --print (not API) -- uses subscription, no cost, enables subagents
- ADR-004: Cursor-based incremental scanning -- mtime comparison, first scan processes all
- ADR-005: Port range 14307-15306 -- avoids collision with beads range 13307-14306

## Open Questions for Plan Phase
- Exact system prompt for the analysis Claude session
- Pattern matching heuristic (title similarity threshold)
- Cron implementation: system crontab vs launchd on macOS
- Whether to support --watch mode (continuous monitoring)
title: drift-watch v1.0: AI conversation drift analyzer

description:
## Goal
Parse conversation logs from Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI into a normalized format.

## Deliverables
- src/readers/types.ts: NormalizedConversation type (source, model, project, sessionId, sessionDate, messages[], toolUses[])
- src/readers/claude.ts: Parse ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl
- src/readers/codex.ts: Parse ~/.codex/sessions/**/*.jsonl + metadata from ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite
- src/readers/gemini.ts: Parse ~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats/*.json
- src/readers/index.ts: discoverConversations(since?: Date) returning all conversations across sources
- Cursor-based filtering: only return conversations with file mtime > since parameter

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each reader has unit tests with fixture JSONL/JSON files
- discoverConversations() returns NormalizedConversation[] from all 3 sources
- Gracefully handles missing source directories (skip + warn)
- Gracefully handles malformed JSONL lines (skip + warn, do not crash)
- Extracts model name, project path, and session date from each source

## Data Format Reference
- Claude Code: ~/.claude/projects/<path>/<session>.jsonl - each line has type (user/assistant), message.model, message.role, message.content, timestamp, cwd, gitBranch
- Codex: ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl - session_meta (id, cwd, model_provider, cli_version), response_item (type, role, content[type,text])
- Gemini: ~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/chats/session-*.json - sessionId, projectHash, startTime, messages[id,timestamp,type,content]
title: E2: Conversation Readers

description:
## Goal
Set up the TypeScript project skeleton with build system, test framework, CLI entry point, and npm publish readiness.

## Deliverables
- package.json with bin entry (drift-watch), type: module, tsup build, vitest test
- tsconfig.json (strict, ESM, Node 20+)
- tsup.config.ts (ESM bundle, entry: src/cli.ts)
- vitest.config.ts
- src/cli.ts: commander-based CLI with placeholder subcommands (init, scan, report, suggest, cron, status)
- .gitignore, .eslintrc or eslint.config.mjs
- npm prepublishOnly: build

## Acceptance Criteria
- pnpm build produces dist/cli.js that runs via node
- pnpm test runs vitest (even if no tests yet)
- drift-watch --help shows all 6 command groups
- drift-watch --version shows package version
- Package can be installed globally via npm link

## Tech Stack
TypeScript, tsup, commander, vitest, zod (runtime validation)
title: E1: Project Scaffold
